lesson.title: The notice that would not stop

paragraphs:
At 08:01, Mara received an overdue notice.
At 08:02, she received another.
By noon, the library had sent 240 of them—one for every minute the book remained late.
Nothing had crashed. The rule was behaving exactly as written, which was unfortunately the problem.

prediction.prompt: What would you inspect first, and what do you expect to find?
prediction.placeholder: Commit to an explanation before seeing the evidence.
prediction.emptyFeedback: Make a prediction before opening the evidence.

paragraphs:
08:01 — the condition became true; BookOverdue was emitted.
08:02 — the condition was still true; BookOverdue was emitted again.
12:00 — the condition was still true; BookOverdue was emitted again.
The clock changed. The rule's eligibility did not.
A time comparison can remain true for hours or years. A rule that only asks whether the book is late has no memory of acting on the answer.
In Allium, the temporal trigger makes the rule eligible. A state guard limits that eligibility to borrowed books; the state change then makes the guard false after one successful evaluation.

workbench.prompt: Repair the rule so a borrowed book becomes overdue exactly once and emits the notice event.
workbench.verificationMessage: Checking the behaviour, not just the syntax…

paragraphs: At 08:01, the borrowed book became overdue and Mara received one notice.
At 08:02, the date condition was still true—but the book was no longer borrowed. The rule was no longer eligible.
The fix was not a faster notification service. It was a more honest description of when the behaviour may happen.
An access grant should expire after its deadline. Write the essential temporal rule using grant.expires_at, guarding the active state and producing expired.
The nouns changed. The behavioural problem did not.

transfer.emptyFeedback: Commit to a transfer answer before checking it.
transfer.verificationMessage: Testing the idea in unfamiliar territory…

completion.headline: You did not memorize a library rule.

paragraphs: You recognized a condition that remains true, guarded it with state, and carried the idea into a different domain. That is the behaviour this lesson set out to teach.

completion.status: Repair and transfer both passed deterministic lesson-intent evaluation.

repair.failureFeedback: Keep the temporal trigger, guard the borrowed state, change the book to overdue, and emit BookOverdue. The incident is resolved only when all four are present.

transfer.failureFeedback: The new domain still needs a temporal condition, a state guard, and an observable state change.
